input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & tools |
| **.NET SDK** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **Android SDK** (via VS Code or standalone) | Bundled with the MAUI workload or [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) | Android build tools & emulators |
| **JDK 17** | [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) | Required by Android tooling |

### 2. Recommended Installation Order

```bash
# 1 — Xcode (accept license after install)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# 2 — .NET SDK (latest LTS, currently .NET 8)
# Download the macOS installer from https://dot.net/download

# 3 — MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui

# 4 — Android SDK / JDK (if not already satisfied)
#     The MAUI workload auto-provisions Android SDK & JDK.
#     To manually manage them, install Android Studio separately.
```

### 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
dotnet --version                  # Confirm SDK version
dotnet workload list              # Confirm 'maui' is installed
dotnet workload search maui       # See available MAUI workload versions
xcodebuild -version               # Confirm Xcode version
java -version                     # Confirm JDK 17+
```

Check the official compatibility matrix at:
**[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)**

### 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-ios

# Run on Android Emulator (create one via Android SDK)
dotnet run -f net8.0-android
```

### 5. IDE Options

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight, free
- **JetBrains Rider** — full-featured, paid

> **Note:** Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the MAUI extension is now the officially recommended editor on macOS.

### Key Gotcha

After macOS or Xcode updates, re-run `sudo xcodebuild -license accept` and `dotnet workload repair` — workloads can break when Apple SDK paths change.
